title: JetBlue has raised checked bag fees. See how much you’ll pay.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue increased checked bag fees by $4 during off-peak and $9 during peak travel periods.
The minimum fee for first checked luggage is now $39 off-peak and $49 peak, with additional charges for late payment.
JetBlue Plus, Business, Premier Cardmembers and TrueBlue Mosaic members retain free checked bag benefits.

title: Mass. man pleads guilty to plotting to assassinate Trump Cabinet member
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ryan Michael English, 24, pleaded guilty to charges related to a plot to assassinate a former Trump Cabinet nominee.
English carried a knife and Molotov cocktails on Capitol grounds, intending to attack then-Treasury Secretary nominee Scott Bessent.
English's plea deal includes charges carrying potential sentences of up to 15 years in prison and fines totaling $260,000.

title: Salisbury woman found guilty of murdering boyfriend with antifreeze
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was found guilty of first-degree murder for poisoning Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in Salisbury in 2022.
Prosecutors argued Church killed Fowler due to jealousy over his affair, using antifreeze-tainted Powerade to administer the poison.
Church faces life in prison, with sentencing scheduled for Thursday after jurors sided with the prosecution's case details.

title: New England nominees for James Beard Awards 2026: See chefs, restaurants who made the cut
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: No Massachusetts chefs or restaurants nominated for the 2026 James Beard Awards, unlike other New England states.
The Shipwright's Daughter in Mystic, CT nominated for Outstanding Chef at the 2026 James Beard Awards.
Winners will be announced June 15 at the James Beard Awards gala in Chicago.

title: Dorchester woman wins $2 million on scratch ticket bought at local liquor store
author: John Waller
bullets: Tuyet Le from Dorchester won $2 million with a scratch ticket from Savin Hill Wine & Spirits.
Le opted for a cash payout, receiving $1.3 million before taxes, and plans to buy a house.
The liquor store that sold the winning ticket was awarded $20,000 by the Massachusetts State Lottery.

title: She was told she might never walk again. Now she’s running Boston.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Caylie McMahon, once unable to walk due to a spinal tumor, is now running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
McMahon and friend Lindsey Stagg are competing as part of Spaulding Rehabilitation’s Race for Rehab team to raise funds.
McMahon's recovery journey included support from Spaulding Rehabilitation, influential in her return to athletics.

title: Patriots opt for line help in latest NFL mock draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots expected to draft Florida defensive tackle Caleb Banks, aiming to bolster their defensive line capabilities.
Caleb Banks impressed scouts with his performance in the Senior Bowl and combine despite past injuries.
Full Patriots mock draft includes selections like Max Iheanachor (OT, Arizona State) and Sam Roush (TE, Stanford).

title: Retired Scituate couple mourned after fatal helicopter crash
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Margaret Rimmler, 65, and Patrick Haskell, 59, died in a sightseeing helicopter crash off Kauai, Hawaii.
A 40-year-old woman also died; four others, including the pilot, were aboard the helicopter operated by Airborne Aviation.
The retired couple from Scituate were remembered for their community engagement and professional contributions in insurance and marketing.

title: Mass. school bus driver fired after allegedly urinating inside bus with students onboard
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 71-year-old Swansea school bus driver was fired for allegedly urinating inside a bus with students onboard.
The incident occurred while the driver was transporting 12 students to Hoyle Elementary School on Monday morning.
Police are investigating the incident; no criminal charges have been filed and the driver’s name remains undisclosed.

title: Everything you need to know about Zach Bryan’s planned Jack Kerouac Center in Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Zach Bryan plans to transform Lowell's St. Jean Baptiste Church into the Jack Kerouac Museum and Performance Center.
The center will feature a museum, performance venue, recording studio, and restaurant dedicated to Jack Kerouac's legacy.
Renovation costs are estimated at $25-$30 million, with fundraising efforts underway including grants and merchandise sales.
